Prosumer classic...post your all time favorite tracks he played below and see you Sunday for one last dance around the block for old times sake ;)

"When I work on a track, I envision people dancing, I just make dance tracks.

I think that the only one reasons why I got into music was because I was so mesmerized by the way it made me feel and I wanted to give that feeling back to people. I didn't want to be famous, I didn't want to make money, all I wanted was 'How do I give this feeling', you know, not thinking about anything, releasing the tension and just dancing for 12 hours…I don't even understand what house is…I don't even understand what house is, when you started catogrizing it, you took the mystery out of it just being dance music. When you started to sub name it, then
the division of people started to happen, people started saying 'I like this', 'No I like that...' Chez on music.
Gerd...some complain about hearing "hits" in panorama. Britney Spears is a hit, this is on the other hand is a straight up underground classic.

Dedicated to Joshua by Discodromo.

"Our community of family and friends are deeply saddened by the loss of ours biggest supporter and vibrant sunshine. We would like to thank him for all the wonderful years and memories he helped to create which has made our journey and those of us around us so much more colorful. There are no words which can describe the feeling of his absence. We will keep moving forward to help give back the magic he put in all of our lives. 

In remembrance we have chosen to cancel our gig at Panorama Bar this evening to pay our private respects to his memory and begin the healing process. We apologize but know that you all understand. 

Nothing made us happier than playing this song for Joshua. We know that this made him feel the same happiness he gave to us. 

Spread the love, the magic, and don't stop the dance....

XX Discodromo"

Tama a couple of weeks back...

There has been a lot of articles written recently about the current generation. As they try to figure us out, some would like to put us in a nice tidy box labeled wasted auto-sync instant gratification button pushers with a borrowed identity who only look for the easy option and can't come up with anything new. To that I say the easy option is to stand on the side line and just box and label. The hard part is to get off your ass, and when that feeling to do a Willy Ninja on it starts to rise up inside and you go take a risk and try and make Berlin burn, be it to celebrate life in a pop video, produce a track and hear it played in panorama, make the best drinks with a smile on your face, join an internet company and help share their music around the world, program that auto sync feature in tracktor or just be your true self and not what someone else wants you to be...that you give it a shot and just remember, "Don't forget to go home" and I don't mean to give up and go back to a life inside a box ;)

Francesco/Mr Ties...Normally not interested in critiquing dj sets like football commentators after a match, but am impressed so much that I feel like saying something about Francesco. He has his own sound, charisma and knows what ingredient go into a party. Think he is at his best when he's not playing functional house, but randomly moving back and forth between disco, funk, house, techno. He's in the vein of the classic djs like Levan and Hardy and for a 24 year old to have developed all this is pretty amazing. In a sea of producer djs, it's great to see someone come up the old school way. As I said, one to watch :)

Track of the year: Dj Sneak - All Over My Face...played loads of times this year, every time caused the place to go crazy, had people swinging off the dj box to the point that the music skipped, just causing a rewind back to the start!

Prosumer...definitely the set of the weekend. Sometimes djs resort too much to the kick drum, they raise up the system and the bass and just beat you over the head with a 4/4 beat. It works to raise the energy but over a long period of time it can get tiring. Prosumer on the other hand keeps the volume low and uses the whole range, vocals, melody, soul...something that only comes with being a resident and knowing the crowd and the system ;)
Tracks of the year: Altern 8 - E-vapor-8...prosumer dropped this one, place went crazy.
